author: Jack Williams
description: Easily sort notes by any subjective criteria - Rank cohorts of notes in your vault by pairwise comparisons using the Glicko rating system. - This plugin has not been manually reviewed by Obsidian staff.

manifest.author: Jack Williams
manifest.description: Easily sort notes by any subjective criteria - Rank cohorts of notes in your vault by pairwise comparisons using the Glicko rating system.
manifest.name: Pairwise Glicko Ranking

name: Pairwise Glicko Ranking

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
# Pairwise Glicko Ranking 1.8.0

This release adds an optional **star rating** frontmatter property, turning your cohort results into a familiar n-star score.

## What's new

- **Star rating frontmatter property:** the plugin can now write a derived n-star rating (e.g. 1-7 [like Kepano uses](https://stephango.com/vault#rating-system)) to frontmatter, based on each note's position in the cohort. Like the other stats, it's optional, has a customisable property name, and can be set globally or overridden per cohort.
  - **Configurable scale:** choose the maximum number of stars, and optionally let the scale start at 0 (e.g. 0-5 instead of 1-5).
  - **Whole or fractional stars:** write integer stars, or allow decimals with configurable precision.
  - **Two mapping modes:**
    - **By rating** spreads notes according to their rating gaps (min-max normalised), preserving how far apart your notes really are.
    - **By rank** spreads notes evenly across the scale by rank position.

## Improvements

- **Tidier cohort options modal:** the per-property "Reset to global default" buttons are now compact icon buttons, reducing visual clutter in the cohort settings.
- **Consistent frontmatter settings everywhere:** the labels and descriptions for every frontmatter property now come from a single source, so the global settings page and the per-cohort options modal always show identical, up-to-date wording.

## Full changelog

https://github.com/WilliamsJack/pairwise-glicko-ranking/compare/1.7.5...1.8.0

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description: # Pairwise Glicko Ranking 1.7.5 A polish release focused on the Settings tab. ## Improvements - **Native Obsidian settings integration:** the settings tab now uses Obsidian's declarative settings API, for built-in settings search support and reducing custom UI maintenance - **Cohort actions from settings:** you can now create new cohorts and start sessions directly from the plugin settings tab ## Compatibility - **Minimum Obsidian version is now 1.13.0** - required for declarative settings support. ## Full changelog https://github.com/WilliamsJack/pairwise-glicko-ranking/compare/1.7.4...1.7.5

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
# Pairwise Glicko Ranking 1.7.4

A minor release addressing code quality warnings from https://community.obsidian.md/.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
# Pairwise Glicko Ranking 1.7.3

A small compatibility and polish release.

## Fixes

- **Better pop-out window compatibility:** internal window handling now uses the active document instead of assuming the main window. This improves reliability for arena controls and layout behaviour when sessions are running in pop-out windows.

## Maintenance

- README updates and release-process tidy-up, including build artifact attestation.
- ESLint and internal code-quality clean-up.

## Full changelog

https://github.com/WilliamsJack/pairwise-glicko-ranking/compare/1.7.2...1.7.3

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
# Pairwise Glicko Ranking 1.7.2

A patch release focused on matchmaking correctness and the stability progress bar.

## Fixes

- **Matchmaker now uses real stats from the start of a session:** the arena preloads note IDs for the whole cohort before selecting the first pair. This lets the information-gain matchmaker immediately use existing ratings and uncertainty (instead of treating notes as "unknown" until they are opened), so the first few comparisons are chosen more accurately.

- **Stability progress bar no longer overestimates convergence:** very stable notes no longer "mask" unstable ones in the progress calculation. Sigma is now clamped at your stability threshold when computing overall stability, preventing the bar from reaching 100% too early while some notes are still highly uncertain.

## Full changelog

https://github.com/WilliamsJack/pairwise-glicko-ranking/compare/1.7.1...1.7.2

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
# Pairwise Glicko Ranking 1.7.1

A reliability, polish, and bugfix release.

## Improvements

- **Autosave cohort settings:** The cohort options modal will now autosave when in "edit" mode, aligning with user expectations and the way Obsidian settings tabs work.
- **More efficient frontmatter rank updates during sessions:** when writing frontmatter for the currently displayed pair, ranks are now computed only for the changed pair (instead of recomputing ranks for the whole cohort every time).
- **Added "Uncertainty" (σ) frontmatter option:** you can now choose to write the Glicko uncertainty value to frontmatter (alongside rating, rank, matches and wins). This is configurable in global defaults and can be overridden per cohort.

## Fixes

- **Rounding consistency:** Rating is rounded before calculations, so that ranks accurately reflect the rating you see in frontmatter.
- **Deleted note handling during sessions:** fixed a bug when a user deleted a note currently being compared resulting in a different note being shown in the session bar and the leaf that contained the deleted file.
- **Prevent accidental repeated undo:** Backspace is now included in the key repeat guards, so holding Backspace will not trigger multiple undo actions.

## Full changelog

https://github.com/WilliamsJack/pairwise-glicko-ranking/compare/1.7.0...1.7.1

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
# Pairwise Glicko Ranking 1.7.0

This release adds optional **post-session reports**, generating a markdown summary of what changed during your rating session (gains/losses, surprises, new entrants, leaderboard snapshots, match log, and more).

## What's new

- **Post-session reports (per cohort):** when a session ends, the plugin can now generate an Obsidian note summarising the session.
  - Includes session duration, match count, unique players, draw rate, new entrants, stability change (sigma delta), biggest gains/losses, most surprising results, leaderboard snapshots, and a match log.
  - Reports are disabled by default - you can enable them per cohort in **Cohort options**.

- **Customisable report templates (placeholders):** reports are generated from a template using `{{glicko:...}}` placeholders.
  - Supports both scalar placeholders (like `{{glicko:match-count}}`) and pre-rendered markdown blocks (like `{{glicko:leaderboard-table}}`).
  - Many list/table placeholders support limiting with `:N` (for example `{{glicko:gains-list:5}}`).

## Improvements

- **Cohort creation/edit flow extended:** Cohort options now include a "Generate post-session report" toggle and per-cohort overrides for folder/name/template.

## Breaking changes / migration notes

- **Plugin ID rename:** the plugin ID has changed to `pairwise-glicko-ranking`.
  - Obsidian will treat this as a *new plugin*, which can make your existing settings/ratings appear "missing" until migrated.

### Migraton

1. Copy (or rename) the plugin data folder:
    - from `.obsidian/plugins/pairwise-elo-ranking/`
    - to `.obsidian/plugins/pairwise-glicko-ranking/`
2. Remove the `WilliamsJack/pairwise-elo-ranking` entry in BRAT settings
3. Add the new URL `https://github.com/WilliamsJack/pairwise-glicko-ranking` to BRAT

## Full changelog

https://github.com/WilliamsJack/pairwise-glicko-ranking/compare/1.6.0...1.7.0

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
# Pairwise Glicko Ranking 1.6.0

A major migration from the classic Elo rating system to the **Glicko rating system**.

## About Glicko-1
Unlike Elo, Glicko tracks both a rating and an uncertainty value ("sigma"). This means the plugin can move new or under-compared notes quickly (high uncertainty), while keeping well-established notes stable (low uncertainty) without you having to tune a K-factor. That uncertainty also feeds directly into the information-gain matchmaker.

The result is that your cohorts converge on a stable ranking much faster.

## What's new
- **Glicko:** Glicko replaces Elo, adding uncertainty ("sigma"/rating deviation/RD), which controls how quickly a note's rating can move.
- **Simplified settings:** With Glicko-1, new/uncertain notes move more, and stable notes move less. Uncertainty replaces the need for a tunable K-factor and other convergence heuristics.
- **Rebrand:** the plugin UI, wording, and defaults have been updated to reflect the Glicko rating system. Commands are now named "Pairwise Glicko Ranking: ...".
- **Reset note rating:** a new command lets you reset the active note back to a fresh baseline ($1500$ rating, full uncertainty), either for one cohort or across all cohorts that contain it.
- **Configurable note ID property name (with migration):** you can now change the name of the property or HTML comment used for note IDs (e.g. `glickoId`). When you change it, the plugin will scan your vault and migrate existing IDs so your rating progress is kept.

## Improvements
- **Progress bar settings are easier to find:** "Highlight surprising results" and "Stability threshold" have been moved out of the accordion and into a dedicated "Progress bar" section in settings.

## Breaking changes / migration notes
- **CSS class renames:** UI classes have been renamed from the `elo-*` prefix to `glicko-*` (e.g. `.elo-session-bar` - `.glicko-session-bar`). If you have custom CSS targeting the old classes, you will need to update it.
- **Defaults renamed to `glicko*`:** default frontmatter property names and the default note ID property now use `glicko...` naming (for new users / new configurations).
- **Backwards compatibility for existing vaults:** to avoid breaking existing ratings, upgraded installations default the note ID property name to "eloId" if you do nothing. You can migrate to "glickoId" from Settings when ready.

Full changelog: https://github.com/WilliamsJack/pairwise-elo-ranking/compare/1.5.0...1.6.0

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
# Pairwise Elo Ranking 1.5.0

This release introduces a new information-gain matchmaker, a progress bar, and a better undo flow.

## What's new
- **Information-gain matchmaking:** pair selection now explicitly targets the most informative comparisons by tracking per-note uncertainty ("sigma") and choosing the pair with the highest expected information gain.
- **Stability progress bar in the arena:** the session bar now includes a thin progress bar showing how close the cohort is to stabilising overall.
- **Surprise highlight:** when you pick an unexpected outcome (relative to current ratings), the progress bar can briefly wobble to flag the surprise (optional), alerting you that you made an unexpected choice (giving you the option to undo it if it was a mistake).

## Improvements
- **Undo now restores the previous pair:** when you undo a match, the arena re-opens that same left/right pair so you can immediately re-evaluate and play the rematch.

## Changes to matchmaking settings (breaking)
- The old "Matchmaking heuristics" settings have been removed (similar-ratings sampling, low-matches bias, upset probes), as this functionality is replaced by information-gain matchmaking.

Full changelog: https://github.com/WilliamsJack/pairwise-elo-ranking/compare/1.4.1...1.5.0

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
# Pairwise Elo Ranking 1.4.1

A small reliability release focused on improved handling of note deletions during an active session.

## Fixes
- The arena will no longer suggest comparisons with empty panes if a note in the cohort is deleted during a session.
- The session will now end automatically if deletions leave fewer than two notes in the cohort.
- If either the currently displayed left or right note is deleted, the arena immediately picks a fresh pair to continue.

Full changelog: https://github.com/WilliamsJack/pairwise-elo-ranking/compare/1.4.0...1.4.1

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
# Pairwise Elo Ranking 1.4.0

This release brings **Obsidian Mobile** app support, with a session layout and controls designed for small screens.

## What's new
- **Mobile/phone support:** the plugin is no longer desktop-only and can now run on Obsidian Mobile.
- **Phone-friendly arena controls:** on phones, the session bar switches to a stacked layout with larger tap targets and a single title line showing which note you are currently viewing.
- **"Switch" + contextual "Win" buttons (phone mode):**
  - **Switch** toggles between the left and right note.
  - **Win** chooses the winner for whichever note is currently visible.
  - **Draw**, **Undo**, and **End** remain available as dedicated buttons.

## Improvements
- **Session layout is automatically adapted on phones:** the arena uses a two-tab workflow on phones (your desktop layout setting is preserved for desktop/tablet).
- **More robust pop-out handling:** when pop-out windows are not available (e.g. mobile), choosing "new window" now falls back cleanly to tabs instead of erroring.

Full changelog: https://github.com/WilliamsJack/pairwise-elo-ranking/compare/1.3.5...1.4.0

![colour_base_example_mobile](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/8bf67e9a-b1d7-470a-bfff-45e940f94a81)

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
# Pairwise Elo Ranking 1.3.5

A small performance and polish release focused on tag cohorts and UI stability.

## Improvements
- **Faster tag cohort creation:** the tag picker now uses Obsidian's `MetadataCache` instead of scanning notes, making the "New: from #tags..." flow much snappier in larger vaults.
- **Tag hierarchy support in cohort matching:** nested tags like `#a/b/c` are now treated as `#a`, `#a/b`, and `#a/b/c` when resolving tag-based cohorts. This aligns with how Obsidian treats nested tags and better matches user expectations.
- **Smoother Cohort options warning UI:** the warning about auto-scroll + synchronised scrolling now slides in and out smoothly, rather than immediately appearing and causing the modal layout to jump.
- **Automated releases:** to provide extra confidence that the published release files are built directly from the open-source code in this repository, releases are now built by a GitHub Actions workflow when a version tag is pushed.

## Breaking Changes
- **CSS class rename:** `.cohort-item-info` has been renamed to `.elo-cohort-item-info` so it can be better targeted by custom CSS. If you have custom snippets or themes targeting the old class, update them to the new name.

Full changelog: https://github.com/WilliamsJack/pairwise-elo-ranking/compare/1.3.4...1.3.5

releases.author: WilliamsJack
releases.description:
# Pairwise Elo Ranking 1.3.4

A workflow and safety release focused on smoother side-by-side comparisons and keeping templates out of your cohorts.

## Improvements
- **Synchronised scrolling (per cohort):** you can now keep the left and right notes scrolled together during a session, making it much easier to compare long notes line-by-line. Enabled per cohort in "Cohort options".
- **Templates folder exclusion:** set a "Templates folder" in settings to automatically exclude those notes from all cohorts (vault, folder, tags, Bases, manual). Prevents Elo IDs and frontmatter stats from being written to templates by accident.

Full changelog: https://github.com/WilliamsJack/pairwise-elo-ranking/compare/1.3.3...1.3.4

releases.author: WilliamsJack
releases.description: # Pairwise Elo Ranking 1.3.3 A reliability and data-integrity release focused on **Elo IDs** - making it easier to keep cohorts clean when notes are copied, and safer to switch where IDs are stored. ## What's new - **Optional Elo ID migration when changing "Elo ID location":** when you switch between storing IDs in **frontmatter** vs the **end-of-note HTML comment**, the plugin can now scan your vault and offer to move existing IDs for you. - **Duplicate Elo ID detection and guided resolution before sessions start:** if two or more notes in your chosen cohort share the same Elo ID (common after duplicating/copying a note), the session will pause and prompt you to choose which note keeps the ID. The ID is removed from the others so they can be assigned fresh IDs as needed. ## Improvements - **More robust HTML comment ID handling:** better parsing and cleanup of end-of-note `` markers, including handling multiple markers and preserving surrounding whitespace more carefully when removing or rewriting them. Full changelog: https://github.com/WilliamsJack/pairwise-elo-ranking/compare/1.3.2...1.3.3

releases.author: WilliamsJack
releases.description:
# Pairwise Elo Ranking 1.3.2

A quality-of-life release focused on reliability and a smoother ranking experience.

## Improvements
- **Suppress keyboard shortcuts in edit mode:** arrow keys actions are suppressed when focus is in a text entry or editor region, allowing you to make quick edits to your notes from the arena.
- **Better "first image" scroll for long notes:** initial scroll now progressively scrolls to force lazy-rendered content to load, so "first image" works much more reliably.
- **Visual feedback for keyboard voting:** when you use keyboard shortcuts, the corresponding session bar button briefly "presses in" to confirm the action.
- **Settings tab now shows the plugin icon:** the Elo settings page displays a trophy icon (temporarily hidden by [Obsidian v1.11.6](https://obsidian.md/changelog/2026-01-26-desktop-v1.11.6/))

## Fixes
- **Bases controller readiness edge case:** fixed a timeout when resolving a Base cohort if the Base is already open elsewhere and `controller.currentFile` stays null - null/undefined is now treated as valid when other readiness signals are present.
- **Prevent accidental multi-votes:** holding down an arrow key no longer triggers repeated voting due to key auto-repeat.
- **Right-split layout in the wrong order:** Fixed notes appearing in the wrong position in "right-split" mode after an Obsidian update.

## Documentation
- Added ideas to try - quick and useful cohort examples.
- Improved motivation and overview paragraphs to give a clearer idea of what the plugin can do for you.
- Updated UI screenshots

Full changelog: https://github.com/WilliamsJack/pairwise-elo-ranking/compare/1.3.1...1.3.2

releases.author: WilliamsJack
releases.description:
# Pairwise Elo Ranking 1.3.1

A maintenance release focused on making **Base cohorts** more reliable.

## Improvements
- **More robust Bases cohort resolution:** Base cohorts are now resolved by opening the `.base` view via Obsidian's link handling and waiting for Bases to finish its initial scan, rather than attempting to manually trigger and instrument query runs.
- **Better compatibility with Bases internal changes:** results extraction no longer assumes as much, reducing breakage risk if Bases alters its internal workings.

Full changelog: https://github.com/WilliamsJack/pairwise-elo-ranking/compare/1.3.0...1.3.1

releases.author: WilliamsJack
releases.description:
# Pairwise Elo Ranking 1.3.0

Bases cohort support is here - define a cohort from a `.base` file and a view, and rank exactly what your Base returns.

## What's new
- **Base cohorts (Obsidian Bases integration):** you can now create a cohort from a specific **Base** and **view**, then run pairwise Elo ranking over the notes returned by that query.

## Improvements
- **Docs tidy-up:** README wording and formatting refreshed (including clearer Bases-focused quick start instructions).

## Fixes
- **Pop-out window layout reliability:** improved handling for the "new window" arena layout so splits occur inside the pop-out window more consistently.

## Compatibility
- **Minimum Obsidian version is now 1.10.0** (required for Bases support).

Full changelog: https://github.com/WilliamsJack/pairwise-elo-ranking/compare/1.2.1...1.3.0

releases.author: WilliamsJack
releases.description:
# Pairwise Elo Ranking 1.2.1

Quicker, more predictable sessions with tidier UI text.

## Improvements
- UI wording refreshed to sentence case and polished (e.g. "frontmatter", "Command palette").
- Fix stuck panes and stray notices when ending sessions or closing pop-outs.
- Frontmatter ID and stats updates, and saves are more dependable.

Full changelog: https://github.com/WilliamsJack/pairwise-elo-ranking/compare/1.2.0...1.2.1

releases.author: WilliamsJack
releases.description:
# Pairwise Elo Ranking 1.2.0

Code quality improvements and bugfixes.

## What's new
- You can now rename the default frontmatter properties even when the property is disabled

## Fixes
- Improved reliability when opening and cleaning up panes, fixing cases where panes could remain attached after ending a session
- Matchmaking now properly avoids immediately repeating the last pair

## Code quality and cleanup
- Cohort handling was consolidated with a registry for consistency and future cohort types, including Bases
- Removed legacy overloads and other old code
- Internal refactors and type safety
- Upgrade to TypeScript 5.9

Full changelog: https://github.com/WilliamsJack/pairwise-elo-ranking/compare/1.1.0...1.2.0

releases.author: WilliamsJack
releases.description: # Pairwise Elo Ranking 1.1.0 This release allows setting an initial scroll position per-cohort. When comparing notes, the editor will automatically scroll to that point to allow for quicker comparisons between what matters most to you. Full changelog: https://github.com/WilliamsJack/pairwise-elo-ranking/compare/1.0.1...1.1.0

releases.author: WilliamsJack
releases.description:
# Pairwise Elo Ranking 1.0.1

- Allow themes and snippets to style the cohorts rows on the Settings page

releases.author: WilliamsJack
releases.description:
# Pairwise Elo Ranking 1.0.0

Initial release of the Pairwise Elo Ranking plugin: Easily sort notes by any subjective standard - Rank cohorts of notes in your vault by pairwise comparisons using the Elo rating system.

## Highlights

- Start quickly from the ribbon icon or Command Palette
- Efficiently review and pick a winner with keyboard shortcuts and an unobtrusive on-screen bar
- Per-cohort stats can be written to frontmatter (highly configurable)
- Advanced convergence and matchmaking heuristics you can tune
  - Elo engine heuristics: provisional boost, optional K decay, upset boost, big‑gap draw boost.
  - Matchmaking heuristics: prefer similar ratings, bias towards fewer matches, occasional large‑gap probes.
- Choose a layout that suits your workflow: reuse active pane, right split, new tab or pop‑out window.
- Robust to renames and moves via stable per-note Elo IDs
- Cohorts are saved so you can resume ranking sessions, picking up where you left off

## Planned

- Bases integration: use Bases to define a cohort (when the API is released).

Compatibility: Obsidian 1.2.7+, desktop only.  

Full Changelog: https://github.com/WilliamsJack/pairwise-elo-ranking/commits/1.0.0
